behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure can come from real git rename detection/CLI flags and subprocess output format, not only local categorization logic. A mocked git response could hide a real bug where the tool invokes git without proper rename detection and therefore never receives rename records. No AI/ML dependency; no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against real git state, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 add/0 delete for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff behavior is tied to real git subprocess output/encoding and real repository contents. In-process fixtures can miss decode/crash paths that only appear with actual binary blobs and git output conventions. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, commit or modify a binary file (e.g., image), run the CLI, assert non-zero binary count appears and process exits successfully without traceback.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git behavior in an unborn-HEAD repo (exit codes/messages from subprocess). Mocking can hide failures in command invocation and error handling around actual git process responses. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert successful exit and an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is end-to-end correctness against actual git history, requiring real commits/renames/deletes and real command execution. Module-only tests can validate counting logic but may miss mismatches introduced by real git query semantics. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known numbers of added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run the CLI, and assert reported counts exactly match the known history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are defined by real git index/worktree state and subprocess queries. Fakes frequently miss subtle differences in git command scope and flags, so real repo + real git is the minimum boundary. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create the same content changes in two scenarios (staged and unstaged), run the CLI in each, and assert identical structured stdout output.
